DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-26-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT JEFF OBERTI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as to the second, third, sixth and seventh causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Saxco International, LLC is a) OVERRULED as to the second cause of action for breach of duty of loyalty, the third cause of action for misappropriation of trade secrets and the seventh cause of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law and b) SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for intentional interference with prospective advantage. The California Uniform Trade Secrets Act "does not displace noncontract claims that, although related to a trade secret misappropriation, are independent and based on facts distinct from the facts that support the misappropriation claim." (Angelica Textile Servs. v. Park, (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 495, 506.) Saxco's allegations that Mr. Oberti solicited fellow employees while working for Saxco and made misrepresentations to Saxco and its customers are sufficiently independent to avoid preemption as to the loyalty and UCL claims. (Complaint 8:22-9:1.) Saxco's intentional interference is based on the same nucleus of facts as the misappropriation claim. (Complaint 14:5-15.) Saxco's misappropriation claim alleges sufficient facts that identify the alleged trade secrets and Mr. Oberti's misappropriation of those trade secrets. (Complaint 9: 16-26; 10:13-19.) Diodes, Inc. v. Franzen (1968) 260 Cal.App.2d 244 is distinguishable as Saxco alleges more than a "secret process." CCP 2019.210 provides that Saxco must sufficiently identify (not necessarily in the complaint) the trade secrets before discovery so any lack of detail is not prejudicial. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
